item.title: Epimer-Dependent Solid-State Landscapes of Trospium and Exotrospium Halide Salts
item.description: Trospium (TR) and exotrospium (ETR) form a closely related epimeric pair of quaternary ammonium compounds, differing only in the stereochemistry of the tropane ring system. While the solid-state chemistry of trospium has been studied mainly for its ...

item.title: Spatial placement of half-Salen-bound nickel single-atoms on MOFs governs cooperative photocatalytic hydrogen evolution
item.description: The integration of single-atom catalysts into metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) offers a promising strategy for coupling molecular catalytic sites with photoactive architectures. While previous studies have focused primarily on the identity and coordination ...

item.title: Half-edge graphs, Lewis Formulas, and Electron Pushing
item.description: Lewis structures are frequently used to display the electronic structure of molecules. Chemical reaction mechanisms, on the other hand, are commonly visualized by means of “curved arrows” that denote the movement of electron pairs or single unpaired ...

item.title: How the Oxidation State Drives Bonding Properties in CeIII and CeIV Tris(amidinate) Complexes
item.description: We report the synthesis and characterization of a pair of homoleptic cerium tris(amidinate) complexes, [CeIII{PhC(NtBu)2}3] and [CeIV{PhC(NtBu)2}3][Al(OC4F9)4], containing cerium in both its trivalent (CeIII) and tetravalent (CeIV) oxidation states. To ...

item.title: Unlocking the potential of Glycosidase-Cleavable AgroDrug Conjugates for Sustainable Agriculture
item.description: The Crop protection industry is currently locked in a race to discover the next generation of efficacious, safe and disruptive modalities. Towards this endeavour, Agrodrug conjugates (AgDCs) offer many unique opportunities to tune the properties of active ...

item.title: Structure and Stability Prediction of Binary and Ternary Molecular Mixed Crystals Using Ensemble Free Energies
item.description: Mixed crystals provide a route to tune the properties of molecular solids through continuous changes in composition. However, predicting their stability is challenging because it requires determining both substitution energies and configurational entropy ...

item.title: Atomically Defined Photoswitchable Hybrid Nanostructures Built from Functionalized Norbornadienes and Cobalt Oxide Nanoislands on Au(111)
item.description: Organic photoswitches on surfaces enable the construction of interfaces that can store solar energy (molecular solar thermal systems, MOST) or change their functional properties in response to light (photoresponsive interfaces). However, many ...

item.title: The Golden Path to Brightness: Unlocking Room-Temperature Rare Earth Luminescence with Phosphinocarboxylate-based RE/Au Complexes
item.description: Rare earth/transition metal (RE/TM) complexes offer unique opportunities to combine complementary electronic properties within a single molecular framework but remain challenging to access because of the distinct coordination preferences of both metal ...

item.title: Fundamentals of Electrochemical Energy Storage: Theory and Practice
item.description: The rechargeable alkali-ion battery has emerged as a transformative technology, revolutionizing portable energy storage in a wide range of applications, from small electronic devices to large electric vehicles. In this review, we combine modern ...

item.title: Wise Co-Solvent Selection for Modulating Bromide Solvation and Improving the Efficiency of Flowless Zn-Br2 Batteries
item.description: Flowless aqueous Zn-Br2 batteries (ZBBs) are attractive for safe, low-cost stationary energy storage, yet remain limited by bromine-related self-discharge and a rate-dependent efficiency trade-off: coulombic efficiency (CE) often increases at high current ...

item.description: Donor-acceptor cyanoarenes are widely employed as photocatalysts, yet their photostability and degradation pathways remain critical areas of investigation. Herein we report that commercially available 3DPA2FBN, upon irradiation with blue light at room ...

item.title: Light-Gated Targeting of DNA Replication Foci in Cancer Cells
item.description: DNA three-way junctions (3WJs) are transient non-canonical DNA structures linked to replication stress, genome instability and cancer, but their controlled recognition remains rare and has not yet been demonstrated in cells. Here we report the first light-...

item.title: Chemical Shift Prediction Beyond the Electronic Structure Limit
item.description: Nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) is a critical tool for developing pharmaceuticals, studying proteins, and identifying natural products. However, many diagnostic chemical shift variations remain too small to be interpreted by computational methods, making ...

item.title: Unlocking the activities of polyglutamylation enzymes in brain tubulin with top-down mass spectrometry

item.description: Rare earth elements (REEs) are essential for advanced energy technologies, electrified transportation, modern electronics, and strategic defense systems. Although the United States possesses substantial domestic REE resources, the nation currently lacks ...

item.title: Green Chemistry, Circular Economy, and Artificial Intelligence: Towards Sustainable and Regenerative Industrial Systems in Algeria
item.description: Green chemistry, formalized in the mid-1990s, provides a design framework for reducing or eliminating hazardous substances, minimizing waste, and improving the overall sustainability of chemical products and processes. In parallel, the circular economy ...

item.title: Highly stable microporous water for energy absorption
item.description: Water has poor compressibility. However, introducing hydrophobic porosity into water can make it highly compressible, as water molecules can be squeezed into the hydrophobic pores under pressure. This offers an exciting opportunity to use water to absorb ...

item.title: Immobilized Thiol Catalysts for Native Chemical Ligation in Flow
item.description: Native chemical ligation (NCL) is a well-established research tool, used to access long and complex peptides that cannot be efficiently synthesized by other means such as linear solidphase peptide synthesis (SPPS). However, its broader application beyond ...

item.title: Reaction-programmed hydrogel formation via base-triggered conversion of a peptide progelator
item.description: Short peptides are promising building blocks for supramolecular soft materials yet controlling when and where hydrogelation occurs remains challenging. Here, we report a reactionprogrammed strategy for peptide hydrogel formation based on a biphasic ...

item.title: Water transport in MOF monoliths under pressure
item.description: Pressurised intrusion into hydrophobic metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) is of considerable interest for energy absorption and fluid transport applications, yet the transport mechanisms in monolithic architectures remain poorly understood. Here, in-situ ...

item.title: A Stable Ruthenium(CAAC-5) Olefin Metathesis Catalyst for Bioconjugation
item.description: Olefin metathesis is a powerful transformation for carbon–carbon bond formation, yet its application in biological settings is challenged by the instability of most ruthenium catalysts in aqueous media. Herein, we report a stable Ru(CAAC-5) olefin ...

item.title: Catalytic Uncaging of Anticancer Drugs via a Buchner Ring-Expansion
item.description: Selective drug activation in living systems remains a central challenge in targeted cancer therapy. Bioorthogonal catalysis addresses this by using abiotic transition-metal catalysts to unmask prodrugs at the tumor site, but the repertoire of suitable ...

item.description: Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) are a family of neutral hydrophobic organic compounds containing a number of fused aromatic rings. Some sources of PAHs include gasoline and diesel engine combustion, wood burning, and pavement sealants. ...

item.description: Rh single-atom catalysts (SACs) have recently attracted significant attention as highly active heterogeneous catalysts for the hydroformylation of olefins. Despite the potential of these systems to become an alternative to the commercial and highly ...

item.title: Fabrication of graphitic carbon nitride nanostructures with tunable morphology for bioimaging
item.description: Graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4) is a versatile material for biomedical, energy, and environmental applications. Bulk g-C3N4is commonly used as a precursor for nanoscale objects, yet systematic links between synthesis conditions, nanostructure ...

item.title: Asymmetric Pulse Electrodeposition of High-Index Faceted Gold Nanostructures for 5-Hydroxymethylfurfural Oxidation
item.description: This work reports the electrodeposition of high-index faceted gold particles resembling rhombic hexecontahedrons in choline chloride urea deep eutectic solvent through the application of asymmetric potential pulses. Potential pulses alternated between ...

item.title: Immobilized Thiol Catalyst for Native Chemical Ligation in Flow
item.description: Native chemical ligation (NCL) is a well-established research tool, used to access long and complex peptides that cannot be efficiently synthesized by other means such as linear solidphase peptide synthesis (SPPS). However, its broader application beyond ...

item.title: A Standard Machine-Learning Virtual Screening Pipeline Produces Candidates Indistinguishable From Random Drug-Like Compounds: A Quantitative Audit Using COX-2 As Case Study
item.description: Motivation: Machine-learning (ML) virtual screening pipelines report strong retrospective benchmark performance (Pearson R > 0.8) and convert it into prioritized candidate lists. Whether those candidates dock better than random drug-like compounds against ...

item.title: Joint analysis of transfer coefficient and proton reaction order exposes kinetic relevance of elementary steps in electrocatalysis
item.description: Mechanistic analysis in heterogeneous electrocatalysis is currently rooted in one of two predominant approaches. Either (i) measured Tafel slopes near cardinal values (30, 40, 60, or 120 mV dec−1) are interpreted to reflect a single rate-determining step ...

item.title: Shear viscosity of liquid Sb and Bi from pseudopotential theory: the temperature-insensitive soft-tail component
item.description: A static, pairwise pipeline, a Bretonnet–Silbert (BS) pseudopotential with Rice–Allnatt transport theory, reproduces the magnitude of liquid-metal shear viscosity, while its temperature gradient marks where the static description reaches its boundary. We ...

item.title: Ion Transport Enhancement in Charge-Regulating Hydrogels
item.description: Weak polyelectrolyte hydrogels are emerging as a versatile platform for ion transport and separation, desalination, and energy applications. However, a predictive understanding how charge regulation influences ionic mobility in these polymer networks ...

item.title: Estimating the make-up of a blend in terms of its parent mixtures (e.g., reverse-engineering a wine blend) based on chemical fingerprints
item.description: In the chemical, food & beverage, agricultural, and pharmaceutical industries, non-reacting complex mixtures are often blended to tune the properties of a finished product. Herein, we tackle the inverse problem of reconstructing the unknown make-up of a ...

item.title: Sisyphus: A Topology-Compiled Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Platform with Structure-Only Input and Bayesian Parameter Refinement
item.description: Physiologically based pharmacokinetic (PBPK) platforms have achieved regulatory acceptance for drug–drug interaction and pediatric dose optimization, yet regulatory-grade use of the dominant implementations typically relies on experimentally measured or ...

item.title: Magnetic Fields Affect Hydroxyl Structure at the Interface of Water and Dysprosium-Treated Alumina
item.description: Resolving magnetic field effects on surface and solution structure at the molecular scale is important to a range of applications, including synthesis, separations, and catalysis. We present direct evidence that paramagnetic rare earth element (REE) ions ...

item.title: Exploring Functional Patterns in Mass Spectrometry Imaging Data with Spatial Pathway and Chemical Class Analysis (SPCCA)
item.description: Mass spectrometry imaging (MSI) has enabled unprecedented advances in spatial mapping of metabolites and lipids within tissues. However, interpretation remains challenging when following common practice of visualizing one ion image at a time. To address ...

item.title: Overcoming Cathode Coating Inhomogeneity: The Role of LiF Interlayer in Enabling Conformal LiNbO3 Protection on LiNi0.8Co0.1Mn0.1O2 for All Solid-State Batteries

item.title: Systematic Errors Begone! Yb(III) Electronic Structure Demands Dynamic Electron Correlations
item.description: Yb(III) complexes are promising targets for molecular spin qubits and near-IR luminescence agents but often struggle to achieve noteworthy single-molecule magnet performance. These properties are highly dependent on the electronic structure generated by ...

item.title: A Computational Study of Gemcitabine Encapsulation in CC3 Porous Organic Cage: Molecular Docking, Molecular Dynamics, Binding Free Energy, and Structure-Activity Relationship Analysis
item.description: Gemcitabine, a first-line nucleoside analogue chemotherapy drug, is rapidly inactivated in plasma by cytidine deaminase (CDA), severely limiting its therapeutic efficacy. Porous organic cages (POCs) have emerged as promising drug delivery vehicles due to ...

item.title: Data Scarcity in Mutant-Specific Drug Design:
item.description: Drug resistance remains a critical challenge in EGFR-targeted cancer therapy, with the T790M gatekeeper mutation accounting for approximately 60% of acquired resistance to firstand second-generation EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKIs). We present an ...

item.description: Combining a robust catalytically active oxygen transport membrane with ceria-based oxides with high oxygen storage capacity has been used to improve catalytic properties, such as the partial oxidation of methane, with the goal of efficient hydrogen ...

item.title: Support-induced charge redistribution governs restructuring trajectories in bimetallic Pt oxidation catalysts
item.description: Supported Pt bimetallic catalysts often exhibit enhanced performance compared to their monometallic counterparts, but their widespread implementation remains challenging due to reaction-driven surface restructuring and phase segregation under reaction ...

item.description: The substantial concentration gap between low-abundance host cell proteins (HCPs) and high-abundance monoclonal antibody drug products presents a significant analytical challenge for HCP analysis using conventional proteomics workflow. Advances in sample ...

item.title: Waste and Peroxide-free Aerobic Oxidative Coupling of Aldehydes and Amines to Amides

item.title: Crystal symmetry engineering of one-dimensional helical perovskites for integrated circularly polarized light detection and bulk photovoltaic effect

item.description: The development of efficient bioelectronic interface for redox enzyme is essential for advancing biosensing and electrocatalytic applications. In this study, we report a gold nanoparticle (Au NPs) modified electrode platform for immobilization and ...

item.title: A fully-polarizable KS-DFT/AMOEBA embedding scheme for plane wave basis sets through the MiMiC framework

item.title: Dual-Function Cosubstrates Enable Threonine Dehydrogenase-Driven Chemoenzymatic Pyrrole Synthesis

item.title: Real-Time, Label-Free Monitoring of Microbial Pectin Degradation and Carbon Assimilation Using Raman Spectroscopy
item.description: Pectin is one of the major polysaccharides produced in plants, and a valuable source of renewable carbon. In this study, we have established a Raman spectroscopy-based framework to study the degradation of pectin by Bacillus subtilis BGN4 and directly ...

item.title: The Snow Hypothesis of the Origin of Life, version 2.0: a quantitative thermal-gradient conveyor with a cryoprotectant
item.description: The Snow Hypothesis proposes that prebiotic chemistry concentrated and organized at the boundary between snow and a warm mineral surface. Version 2.0 develops this into a quantitative “thermal-gradient conveyor” model and revises one key point: the ...

item.title: Introducing Redox-Active Residues to Enhance P450 Peroxygenase Performance

item.title: Controlling Interfacial and Bulk Charge Transport in Exsolution Catalysts for High-Activity Water Oxidation

item.title: Structure Prediction of Multi-Component Molecular Crystals with Genarris 4.0
item.description: Multi-component molecular crystals are increasingly used in pharmaceuticals, organic electronics, and energetic materials because they can have unique, tunable properties that are not achievable in single-component crystals. Predicting the structure of ...

item.title: On Contemporary Tafel Analysis
item.description: Tafel slope analysis has been the cornerstone of mechanistic interpretation in multi-step electrochemical reactions for nearly a century, underpinning our understanding of reactions from hydrogen evolution to carbon dioxide reduction. Here we demonstrate ...

item.title: Intrinsic Irreducible-Cartesian Tensor Carrier for Equivariant Interatomic Potentials
item.description: Equivariant interatomic potentials use compact angular irreps, while many observables and long-range sources are Cartesian tensors. We introduce the intrinsic irreducible-Cartesian tensor carrier (ICTC), which builds harmonic polynomial bases and ...
